heading: Action by owner for limitation
label: 30529 Action by owner for limitation
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)3051146 App.:185.R.S. § 4285; , .June 5, 1936, ch. 521, § 349 Stat. 1480In subsection (a), the words “bring a civil action . . . in a district court of the United States” are substituted for “petition a district court of the United States” for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the United States Code. See rule 2 of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure (28 App. U.S.C.). The words “of competent jurisdiction” are omitted as unnecessary.In subsection (b), the word “pending” before “freight” is added for consistency in the chapter. The words “to carry out this chapter” are substituted for “to carry out the provisions of section 183 of this Appendix” because of the reorganization of the source provisions.
Amendments2022— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 117–263section 30511 of this title

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: The owner of a vessel may bring a civil action in a district court of the United States for limitation of liability under this chapter. The action must be brought within 6 months after a claimant gives the owner written notice of a claim.

children.label: A
children.text: an amount equal to the value of the owner’s interest in the vessel and pending freight, or approved security; and

children.label: B
children.text: an amount, or approved security, that the court may fix from time to time as necessary to carry out this chapter; or

children.label: A
children.text: the owner’s interest in the vessel and pending freight; and

children.label: B
children.text: an amount, or approved security, that the court may fix from time to time as necessary to carry out this chapter.

children.heading: .—Cessation of Other Actions
children.label: c .—Cessation of Other Actions
children.text: When an action has been brought under this section and the owner has complied with subsection (b), all claims and proceedings against the owner related to the matter in question shall cease.
